in full, followed by the initial of a middle name (Luke J. Parker). This is •    Americans are often addressed (and sign their names) with the first name •    Dates are written differently (month before day) ►    324.

12 American usage is different from British in some ways: opening and closing formulae without commas or full stops.

11  British people now usually write abbreviated titles, initials, addresses, dates, and (Mrs Angela Brookes), or you can write one or more initials (Mrs A E Brookes).

title (Mr, Mrs, etc) before the name. You can write the first name in full 10 On the envelope, put the first name before the surname. People usually write a Luke Forbes Yours sincerely above the full printed name: signature. Friendly business letters are often signed with the first name only In a formal printed letter, add your full printed name after your handwritten L Forbes, L J Forbes.

writing any title (Mr/Ms/Dr, etc). Ways  of writing one’ s full name: Luke Forbes, 9  Sign with your first name (informal) or your full name (formal), but without kind regards.

and Cordially. Other less common formulae are (With) best wishes and (With) In American English, common endings are Sincerely, Sincerely yours, Yours truly name (e.g. Dear Ms Hawkins, Dear Peter Lewis) usually finish Yours sincerely.

Yours faithfully in British English. Formal letters which  begin with the person’ s 8  Letters which  begin with formulae like Dear Sir(s) or Dear Madam usually finish most common in Britain.) from the left. Do  the same for each new paragraph. (The first method is now the Dear X and start again on the left, or start again on the next line, a few spaces A  colon (:) is used in American business letters. Either leave an empty line after 7  After Dear X, put a comma  or nothing at all, not an exclamation mark (!).

Formal emails are similar in style to letters on paper.
